changelog_v2: Added all 18 helper lipid identities from Figure S2
Added SMILES for all 18 helper lipids (PubChem verified where possible)
Added Neu-LNP exact composition from Figure S15a
Added An-LNP exact composition from Figure S15c
Confirmed per-formulation barcode data exists in S6 tables (144 rows with numeric delivery data)
Added Kauffman LNP comparison data from Figure S14
Corrected: S6 tables show only C14PEGK and C18PEGK — confirms 2 PEG lipids
Added 19 cell type flow markers from Figure S3b
Confirmed DSPC = 18:0 PC = N7 in the neutral library
Added headgroup size SAR detail: DOPE vs MonoMethylPE vs DiMethylPE vs DOPC

paper.title: The Transcriptional Response to Lung-Targeting Lipid Nanoparticles in Vivo
paper.authors: Afsane Radmand, Melissa P. Lokugamage, Hyejin Kim, Curtis Dobrowolski, Ryan Zenhausern, David Loughrey, Sebastian G. Huayamares, Marine Z. C. Hatit, Huanzhen Ni, Ada Del Cid, Alejandro J. Da Silva Sanchez, Kalina Paunovska, Elisa Schrader Echeverri, Aram Shajii, Hannah Peck, Philip J. Santangelo, James E. Dahlman

ionizable_lipid.name: cKK-E12
ionizable_lipid.transfer_learning_value: HIGH — known SMILES, bridges to external datasets

helper_lipids_tested.name: DOTAP
helper_lipids_tested.notes: Used in SORT paper (Cheng 2020) and Kauffman comparison LNP. Third of 3 cationic helpers in enrichment ranking.

helper_lipids_tested.name: DOTMA
helper_lipids_tested.notes: Ether analog of DOTAP — more metabolically stable. Second most enriched cationic helper overall; highest in some non-EC lung cell types.

helper_lipids_tested.name: 18:0 DDAB
helper_lipids_tested.notes: TOP ENRICHED cationic helper in lung ECs. In other lung cell types (DCs, T, B, Monocytes), DOTMA can be equally or more enriched. Overall ranking across tissues: DDAB > DOTMA > DOTAP. Used in Cat-LNP lead.

helper_lipids_tested.name: DOPC
helper_lipids_tested.headgroup_size_rank: LARGEST (4th of 4 in headgroup SAR comparison)

helper_lipids_tested.name: DOPE
helper_lipids_tested.headgroup_size_rank: SMALLEST (1st of 4 in headgroup SAR comparison — POSITIVELY enriched)

helper_lipids_tested.name: 18:0 PC (DSPC)
helper_lipids_tested.notes: Labeled '18:0 PC' in S2 = DSPC. Positively enriched in liver/spleen. Used in Neu-LNP lead. Standard clinical helper lipid.

helper_lipids_tested.name: 18:1 Caproylamine PE
helper_lipids_tested.tails: 2× C18:1 + caproylamine cap on PE headgroup
helper_lipids_tested.notes: Amide-modified PE headgroup. Need to verify exact SMILES from Avanti catalog.

helper_lipids_tested.name: 18:1 PA
helper_lipids_tested.notes: TOP ENRICHED anionic helper in ALL liver/spleen cell types. Used in An-LNP lead.

helper_lipids_tested.name: 18:1 Biotinyl PE
helper_lipids_tested.tails: 2× C18:1 + biotin conjugate on PE headgroup
helper_lipids_tested.headgroup: biotinylated phosphoethanolamine (anionic due to biotin conjugation removing amine charge)
helper_lipids_tested.notes: Biotin-conjugated PE — unusual anionic helper. Large headgroup with biotin moiety.

headgroup_size_SAR.comparison_set: Four neutral helpers with identical C18:1 dioleoyl tails but different headgroup methylation states
headgroup_size_SAR.conclusion: Smaller headgroups (fewer methyl groups) → more enriched in top-performing LNPs. Consistent with lipid packing parameter theory — smaller headgroups favor inverted hexagonal phase / endosomal escape.

Neu-LNP.tropism: Liver-targeting. Significant delivery to all liver cell types (ECs, DCs, Kupffer, B cells, hepatocytes). Also splenic DCs, kidney ECs/monocytes.

An-LNP.tropism: Liver-targeting. Highest delivery to liver ECs, followed by DCs, Kupffer cells, B cells, hepatocytes.

Kauffman_LNP_comparison.note: Prior lung-targeting LNP from Kauffman et al. 2018 (ref 13), tested alongside Cat-LNP at 0.3 mg/kg
Kauffman_LNP_comparison.result: Cat-LNP (DDAB) significantly outperformed Kauffman LNP (DOTAP) in heart and kidney EC delivery at 0.3 mg/kg

per_formulation_data._note: CRITICAL: Supplementary Figure S6 contains FULL per-formulation tables for all 3 screens

columns: Average normalized delivery (across all cell types)

neutral_screen.lead_lnp_id: Row 32, barcode CCGCAGAG (DSPC, highest avg normalized delivery, red-boxed in figure)
neutral_screen.note: Table embedded as image in PDF. Numeric values would need OCR or manual digitization for ML training.

cationic_screen.lead_lnp_id: LNP #11 (DDAB, R3, C14PEG2K, highlighted in red = Cat-LNP, avg = 7.06)

per_formulation_data.digitization_priority: HIGH — these tables contain per-formulation delivery data for 144 LNPs across lung/liver/spleen. OCR or manual extraction would yield ~430 delivery data points for transfer learning.

key_corrections_from_supplement.detail: 18 helpers × 4 ratios × 2 PEG lipids = 144. Per-screen: 24 cationic (23 passed QC), 64 neutral (59 passed), 56 anionic (55 passed) = 137 injected.
C1=DOTAP, C2=DOTMA, C3=18:0 DDAB. Previous annotation only had DDAB named; now all 3 confirmed from S2.

key_corrections_from_supplement.item: DDAB ranking vs DOTAP confirmed quantitatively
key_corrections_from_supplement.detail: Figure S8b-d shows fold enrichment for all 3 cationic helpers. DDAB is highest in lung ECs. In other lung cell types (DCs, T cells, B cells, Monocytes), DOTMA can match or exceed DDAB. Overall ranking: DDAB > DOTMA > DOTAP.

Figure S9 shows 18:1 PA is top enriched anionic helper. Ranking includes PA, PS(DOPS), Biotinyl PE, Phosphatidylethanol, Phosphatidylpropanol, Phosphatidylmethanol, and (Δ9-Cis) PG. 18:1 PA consistently highest.

Figure S10 shows DSPC and DOPE as top enriched neutral helpers for liver. DOPC, 18:1 Lyso PC, and others lower. The figure x-axis labels include: DOPE, DSPC, DOPC, 18:1 Lyso PC, 18:1 Dimethyl PE, Monomethyl PE, 18:1 Lyso PE, 18:1 Cap PE (Caproylamine PE).

key_corrections_from_supplement.item: Neu-LNP uses molar ratio R4, not the same as Cat-LNP
key_corrections_from_supplement.detail: Neu-LNP: cKK-E12 20% / DSPC 27.5% / Chol 50% / C14PEG2000 2.5% (R4). This is the most cholesterol-rich, lowest-IL ratio. Interesting that the best neutral LNP comes from the high-cholesterol formulation.
An-LNP: cKK-E12 60% / 18:1 PA 12% / Chol 26% / C14PEG2000 2% (R1). Highest IL ratio with lowest helper content.
Cat-LNP, Neu-LNP, and An-LNP all use C14PEG2000 (not C18PEG2000). This could indicate C14PEG is generally preferred, or could be coincidence.

key_corrections_from_supplement.item: Kauffman comparison LNP uses DOTAP (not DDAB)
key_corrections_from_supplement.detail: The prior lung-targeting LNP from ref 13 uses cKK-E12/DOTAP/Chol/C14PEG2000. Cat-LNP (DDAB) outperformed it, supporting DDAB > DOTAP ranking from enrichment analysis.

value_assessment.transfer_learning_rows: Up to 144 (from S6 tables, if digitized)
value_assessment.transfer_learning_value: HIGH — cKK-E12 has known SMILES, all 18 helpers have structures in S2, all compositions fully disclosed
value_assessment.helper_lipid_smiles_library: 16/18 SMILES confirmed (N8 Caproylamine PE and A7 Biotinyl PE need Avanti catalog verification)
value_assessment.feature_engineering_value: HIGH — 4 molar ratios × 2 PEG lipids give dose-response data across IL%, chol%, helper%, PEG identity
next_steps: PRIORITY 1: OCR/digitize the S6 tables to extract all 144 per-formulation delivery values
PRIORITY 2: Verify N8 (Caproylamine PE) and A7 (Biotinyl PE) SMILES from Avanti Polar Lipids catalog
PRIORITY 3: Add these 144 rows to the transfer learning pool with cKK-E12 SMILES as ionizable_lipid feature
PRIORITY 4: Cross-reference DOTAP/DOTMA/DDAB enrichment rankings with Radmand 2024 PNAS (196 LNPs, cationic focus)
PRIORITY 5: Compute molecular descriptors (MW, logP, HBD, HBA, TPSA) for all 18 helper lipids from SMILES
